# Damask

> Self-hosted digital asset management for creative teams that own their data.

Damask is an open-source DAM system you run on your own server. It replaces
cloud DAM services (Bynder, Brandfolder, Canto) for teams that want full data
ownership: all files, all metadata, and all audit history stay on infrastructure
you control. A single Go binary with no external runtime dependencies. SQLite by
default; the storage layer is an interface — swap in a local path or an
S3-compatible bucket in config, no code changes.

## Docs

- User guide: /docs/help
- Self-hosting guide: /docs/tech
- API reference: /docs/tech/api
- Installation: /docs/tech/installation
- Configuration: /docs/tech/configuration
- Upgrading: /docs/tech/upgrade

---

## Who it's for

**Solo creators** — One person, thousands of files. Damask replaces a folder
structure that stopped making sense. Fast upload, instant search, and a clean
way to find the right version of anything.

**Creative teams** — Designers, photographers, video editors working on shared
projects. Roles and permissions keep work organized without locking people out.
Everyone sees what they need, nothing more.

**Studios and agencies** — Client deliverables, brand assets, production
libraries. Damask tracks who uploaded what, when, and what changed. Share work
with clients without giving them an account. Keep the audit trail in-house.

---

## Data model

### Workspace

The top-level tenant. One Damask instance can serve multiple isolated
workspaces. Every row in the database is scoped to a workspace_id. Members
have one of three roles: owner, editor, or viewer.

### Project

Groups assets by client, campaign, or shoot. Color-coded in the sidebar.
Projects are workspace-scoped. An asset belongs to exactly one project.

### Folder

Two-level hierarchy inside each project (project → folder → asset). Assets
can be dragged between folders or dragged to root (no folder).

### Asset

A file — image, video, audio, document, or any binary. Core fields: id,
workspace_id, project_id, folder_id, original_filename, mime_type, size,
created_at, updated_at, deleted_at (soft delete). Each asset tracks its full
version history. The "current" version is the most recently uploaded one
unless a previous version has been manually restored.

### Asset version

A new file uploaded to an existing asset. The previous version is preserved.
Fields: id, asset_id, workspace_id, storage_key, size, content_hash,
created_by, created_at, deleted_at. Rollback is one click (sets the current
version pointer). Share links always point to the current version unless pinned
to a specific version_id.

### Variant

A non-destructive derivative of an asset. The source asset is never modified.
Variants are created by background jobs and stored under their own storage_key.
A variant carries: id, asset_id, workspace_id, type (resize / crop / convert /
watermark / bg_remove / video_transcode / video_frame / audio_transcode /
audio_normalize), status (pending / processing / ready / failed), storage_key,
mime_type, size, params (JSON of transform parameters), title (optional
human-readable label), is_shared (boolean: expose on share links).

Variant types and their parameters:
- **resize** — width, height, fit (cover / contain / fill / inside / outside)
- **crop** — x, y, width, height; or smart_crop: true for content-aware region
- **convert** — format (jpeg / png / webp / avif), quality
- **watermark** — overlay asset_id, position (top-left / top-right / bottom-left
  / bottom-right / center), opacity (0–1), scale (0–1)
- **bg_remove** — provider (remove_bg); requires API key in workspace settings
- **video_transcode** — container (mp4), resolution (e.g. 1080p), strip_audio
- **video_frame** — timestamp_seconds (float)
- **audio_transcode** — format (mp3 / aac / wav / flac / ogg), bitrate_kbps
- **audio_normalize** — target_lufs (default −23, EBU R128)

### Tag

Workspace-scoped labels on assets. Auto-created on first use. Tags have an
optional group_name for visual grouping. System tags (prefixed `_`) are
reserved: `_watermark` marks the workspace watermark asset, `_template` marks
template assets. System tags cannot be deleted or renamed via the API.

### Collection

A cross-project named set of assets. Membership is purely additive — removing
an asset from a collection never touches the original asset. An asset can
belong to many collections. Collections are workspace-scoped and flat (no
nesting). Collections are a valid share target.

### Share

A public link for any asset, project, or collection. No recipient account
required. Fields: id, workspace_id, target_type (asset / project / collection),
target_id, token (URL-safe random), password_hash (optional), expires_at
(optional), allow_comments (boolean), allow_download (boolean). Recipients can
view and leave comments without a Damask account. Shared variants (is_shared =
true) appear in share galleries.

### Ingress source

An automated ingest pipeline that pulls files into a workspace on a schedule
or via webhook. Supported types: email (per-workspace unique ingest address),
sftp, webdav, s3 (S3-compatible), google_drive (OAuth), canva (OAuth). Each
source maps to a destination folder. Ingest log tracks every item: remote_id,
filename, asset_id, status (pending / imported / skipped / error).

### Workflow

An automation graph (trigger → conditions → actions) stored as a JSON node
graph. Supported triggers: asset_uploaded, asset_tagged, asset_version_added,
schedule (cron), webhook (HMAC-verified inbound HTTP). Supported actions:
create_variant, add_tag, move_to_folder, share, notify (webhook). Condition
nodes filter by MIME type, tag, project, file size, etc. Workflow runs are
logged with per-step status, timing, and error details.

### Custom field

User-defined metadata fields per workspace. Types: text, number, date,
single-select, multi-select. Fields are ordered, filterable, and indexed in
FTS5. Up to 50 field definitions per workspace. Values are per-asset.

### Text track

A textual representation of an asset: OCR output, AI-generated description,
subtitle/caption file, transcription, or manual notes. Each track has a source
(ocr / ai_description / subtitle / transcription / manual), a language (ISO
639), plain content stored in the DB row for direct FTS, and an optional
companion file for structured formats (hOCR, SRT, VTT).

### Audit log

Append-only record of every metadata change: asset renames, retags, project
moves, field edits, version uploads and restores, share events, member changes.
Each entry carries: id, workspace_id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id,
diff (JSON of previous/next values), created_at. Configurable retention period.

---

## Self-hosting

**Requirements** — A Linux server (or local machine), Docker or a compiled
binary, and a writable storage path. No external databases, no message
brokers, no caches required.

**What you keep** — All files. All metadata. All audit history. No usage caps,
no per-seat pricing tiers, no data retention clauses.

**Environment variables (key ones)**
- PORT — HTTP port (default 3000)
- DB_PATH — path to SQLite file
- STORAGE_PATH — root path for file storage
- APP_SECRET — secret for token signing and config encryption (required)
- APP_URL — public base URL (used in share links and email)
- STORAGE_BACKEND — "local" (default) or "s3"
- S3_BUCKET, S3_ENDPOINT, S3_ACCESS_KEY, S3_SECRET_KEY — S3 config
- SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS — email for invites
- OIDC_ISSUER_URL, OIDC_CLIENT_ID, OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET — OIDC SSO
- GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET — Google OIDC + Drive ingress
- DEMO_MODE — set to "true" to enable the public demo environment

**Schema migrations** run automatically at startup via golang-migrate.
No manual steps required on upgrade.

**Docker**
```
docker run -d \
  -e APP_SECRET=changeme \
  -e DB_PATH=/data/damask.db \
  -e STORAGE_PATH=/data/files \
  -v /your/data:/data \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  ghcr.io/damask/damask:latest
```

---

## Tech stack

- Backend: Go + Fiber (single static binary)
- Frontend: SvelteKit + TypeScript + Paraglide i18n
- Database: SQLite with FTS5 via modernc.org/sqlite (CGO-free)
- Query layer: sqlc-generated typed functions
- Auth: Paseto v4 local tokens (bcrypt passwords)
- File storage: local filesystem or S3-compatible (afero-based interface)
- Job queue: in-process, SQLite-backed, no external broker
- Observability: OpenTelemetry traces + metrics (OTLP export)
- Image processing: libvips (via bimg) for variants
- Video/audio processing: FFmpeg (shell, CGO-free)
- OCR: Tesseract (shell, CGO-free)
